title: Allowances

meta.description: Workplace allowances — what they are, types of allowances, and when employees are entitled to them.

content:
Allowances are additional payments made to employees on top of their ordinary pay. They compensate employees for particular tasks they perform, the use of special skills, or for expenses incurred as part of their work.

Allowances are set by the applicable Modern Award or registered enterprise agreement. The Pay and Conditions Tool (PACT) can be used to identify what allowances apply.

Types of allowances:

1. Expense-based allowances — reimburse employees for work-related costs:
   - Meal allowance: paid when an employee is required to work overtime for a specified period (commonly 1.5–2 hours)
   - Tool allowance: paid when employees must supply their own tools for work
   - Uniform/clothing allowance: paid when employees must purchase or maintain required uniforms/PPE
   - Vehicle/travel allowance: paid when employees use their own vehicle for work or travel to other locations
   - Phone allowance: paid when employees use personal phones for work purposes

2. Task and responsibility allowances — for performing specific duties:
   - First aid allowance: for employees with first aid qualifications who are required to use them at work
   - Leading hand/supervisory allowance: for employees who supervise or lead other workers
   - On-call allowance: for employees required to be available to work outside ordinary hours
   - Height/disability allowance (building/construction): for working in specific conditions

3. Shift and location allowances:
   - Shift allowance: for working irregular or rotating shifts
   - Broken shift allowance (SCHADS): for employees required to work split shifts with a break
   - Remote location allowance: for working in isolated areas

Uniforms, vehicles and travel:
As part of their job, employees may be required to wear uniforms, drive vehicles, or travel to other locations. In these situations an employee may be entitled to:
- Reimbursement for the cost of the uniform (or provision of the uniform by the employer)
- Payment for travel time to work sites (award-dependent)
- Kilometre-based reimbursement for use of personal vehicle

Award-specific allowances:
Allowances vary significantly between awards. For example:
- Building and Construction Award: tool allowance, meal allowance after 1.5 hours of overtime
- Road Transport Award: meal allowance for overtime worked continuously for 2+ hours
- SCHADS Award: meal allowance, first aid, on-call, broken shift allowance

Important for payslips:
Any allowance paid must be separately identified on the employee's pay slip, including the amount and nature of the allowance.
tables: Compensate for meal costs during overtime
Use of personal vehicle for work
Employee drives own car for work
Qualified first aider on call at work
Employee holds first aid cert and is designated
Employee required to be contactable off-shift
Employee leads a team of workers

key_facts.legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth); individual Modern Award allowances clauses
